question.text: Which of the following is a characteristic of OTC (Over-the-Counter) derivative contracts?

answer.text: They are customized contracts with counterparty credit risk
They are standardized contracts guaranteed by a clearing corporation
They require daily mark-to-market margin payments
They can only be settled through physical delivery

explanation.text: **The Logic:** OTC derivatives are customized contracts negotiated directly between two parties. Since there is no exchange or clearing corporation intermediary, they carry counterparty credit risk — the risk that one party may default on its obligations. **The Trap:** Standardization and clearing corporation guarantee are features of exchange-traded derivatives. Daily mark-to-market margins are also an exchange-traded feature. Both OTC and exchange-traded contracts can be settled in cash or by physical delivery. **Cross-Exam Context:** The OTC vs. exchange-traded distinction is tested across all NISM derivative exams. **Key Takeaway:** OTC derivatives: customized, bilateral, counterparty risk, no margins, flexible terms. Exchange-traded: standardized, clearing corp, no counterparty risk, margined.
